title: Archive: LEAP-Hand Glove / Standalone Hand Hardware
description: Archive reason: LEAP Hand and related embodiment-generalization work already cover the generic low-cost dexterous-hand hardware angle; live value belongs in Open-Hardware scoring or VTLA sensor choices.
summary: Standalone LEAP-hand glove/hardware ideas are archived so they do not duplicate the Open-Hardware Mobile Collection Survey. Keep only the useful build, embodiment, and tactile-sensor lessons.

details.text: LEAP Hand already establishes a low-cost anthropomorphic robot hand for robot learning, and GET-Zero extends the hand-hardware variation angle for zero-shot embodiment generalization. That makes a standalone LEAP-hand/glove card redundant for this dashboard.

details: Moved-out live work: Open-Hardware Mobile Collection should score whether any hand/platform is worth building; VTLA should track tactile sensor and glove data value.
Archive decision: do not keep a separate project unless we commit to building or buying a specific hand with an owner, BOM, and validation demo.

Notion migration addendum 2026-05-31: UMI hand-vitac / DEXO-like exoskeleton capture and tactile glove data value were moved out to VTLA and Open-Hardware; only the standalone LEAP-hand glove card stays archived because the original note says the generic direction was scooped.
risks_decisions: Reason type: externally covered hardware family and internal duplication with open-hardware survey.
Reopen condition: a lab purchase/build decision requires a dedicated LEAP-style hand implementation card.
